units.fed_taxes_paid: $ billion (estimated federal taxes paid by state residents + businesses)

perCapitaGsp.name: BEA · GDP by State ÷ Census pop

ownTax.name: Census · Annual Survey of State Govt Finances

fed_transfers.name: Treasury · USAspending.gov + Pew Federal Funds tracker

_meta.notes: GSP figures are current-dollar nominal (BEA SAGDP9N table). Federal transfers + federal taxes paid are policy-attribution estimates — the Rockefeller Institute / Pew methodologies differ but most yield similar rankings (NY, NJ, MA, CA, CT, IL as biggest net donors; NM, MS, KY, WV, AK as biggest net recipients). Estimates here approximate the Rockefeller/Pew range; treat as ±10%.

pros: World's 5th-largest economy if a country. Tech + entertainment + agriculture + biotech — most diversified state economy.
Largest absolute state own-tax revenue. Highly progressive income tax captures Silicon Valley booms.
Massive federal R&D footprint (NASA, DoE labs, defense contractors).
cons: ~$30 B more in federal taxes paid than transfers received — largest absolute net donor.
Wealth concentration: top 1% pays ~half of state income tax → revenue volatility tracks tech IPO cycles.
Housing affordability crisis driving out-migration to Texas + Arizona.

pros: No state income tax — relies on property tax + sales tax + oil severance.
Fastest absolute population growth + corporate relocations (Tesla, Oracle, HP).
Oil + gas + petrochemicals + Permian Basin shale — energy backbone.
cons: Property tax burden among highest in US — regressive vs income tax states.
ERCOT electric grid isolation: 2021 winter storm blackouts cost ~$130 B.
Voter-suppression + abortion litigation = federal investigation overhead.

pros: NYC = global financial capital. Wall Street + media + UN = huge federal tax base.
Strong own-tax effort + progressive structure (top marginal 10.9%).
Highest federal R&D + defense spend per capita on Northeast corridor.
cons: ~$150-200 B net contribution to federal system every year (Rockefeller).
Outmigration: lost 1 House seat in 2020 reapportionment.
Subway + airport infrastructure has structural underinvestment given tax base.

pros: No state income tax. Tourism + retirees + Latin America trade gateway.
Population gainer — net interstate migration from NY/NJ/IL.
Diverse beyond tourism: aerospace, biotech, ports (Miami, Jacksonville, Tampa).
cons: Hurricane exposure: structural fiscal risk for state property insurance backstop.
Inadequate state services (lowest per-capita state spend on K-12 nationally).
Aging population strains Medicaid + long-term care.

pros: Chicago = financial + logistics + commodities hub.
Most diversified Midwest economy — manufacturing + finance + agtech.
Strong research universities (UChicago, Northwestern, UIUC) anchoring tech corridor.
cons: Worst public pension underfunding in US (~$144 B unfunded liability).
Population decline + corporate departures (Boeing, Citadel HQ moved).
Property tax rates among nation's highest, driven by pension deficit.

pros: Eds + meds + manufacturing — diversified across Philadelphia, Pittsburgh, Scranton.
Marcellus Shale natural gas — second-largest US gas producer.
Strong port (Philadelphia) + intermodal logistics infrastructure.
cons: Aging population (one of oldest median ages in US).
Pension funding gaps mirror Illinois pattern, smaller scale.
Pittsburgh's industrial decline still working through; some regions stagnant.

pros: Manufacturing backbone, Intel chip fab + Honda + Ford footprint.
Strong public university system (Ohio State + 13 others).
Logistics hub — Columbus is rapidly growing tech + logistics center.
cons: Rust Belt structural decline — Cleveland, Dayton stagnant outside specific clusters.
Opioid crisis epicenter — high public-health burden.
Education attainment gap with northeast/west coast widening.

pros: Atlanta = aviation hub (busiest US airport) + Fortune 500 cluster (Coca-Cola, Home Depot, Delta).
Film + entertainment industry: 'Y'allywood' rivals Hollywood for production days.
Growing Korean + Latin American immigrant entrepreneurial base.
cons: Rural/urban divide stark — outside Atlanta metro, indicators lag.
Public health investment weak — Medicaid non-expansion.
Voter-access litigation a recurring federal liability.

pros: Research Triangle (RTP) — biotech + tech + research universities (Duke, UNC, NC State).
Charlotte = second-largest US banking center after NYC (Bank of America + Truist).
Manufacturing diversification beyond textiles into aerospace, pharma.
cons: Hurricane exposure on coast; flooding from Florence (2018) etc.
Rural east of state lags economically; opioid + tobacco-belt transition.
School funding equity litigation (Leandro) ongoing for 30+ years.

pros: No state income tax + tech wages = Seattle is Bay Area North.
Boeing + Microsoft + Amazon + Costco + Starbucks HQs.
Highest wages outside Northeast; major Asia-Pacific trade gateway.
cons: Tax structure regressive (no income tax + sales tax) — capital gains tax passed 2021 but litigated.
Seattle homelessness + housing affordability widely covered as policy failure.
Boeing concentration risk — Renton/Everett job concentration.

pros: Big Three auto OEMs (Ford, GM, Stellantis) — automation + EV transition centerpiece.
Great Lakes water access is a long-term strategic asset.
Anchor universities (UMich, MSU) drive automotive R&D.
cons: Detroit recovery uneven; auto industry electrification = job restructuring.
Flint water crisis (2014) damaged public trust; ongoing remediation cost.
Population stagnation — only state to lose population in 2010 Census decade.

pros: DC suburbs (NoVa) = federal contractor capital — Booz Allen, GD, NoGD, Raytheon clusters.
Data center alley (Loudoun County) = ~70% of world's internet traffic transits here.
Strong eds + meds + ports (Norfolk = largest US Navy base + cargo).
cons: Federal contracting concentration = vulnerable to budget cuts / shutdowns.
Hampton Roads sea-level rise: military base relocation risk.
School equity litigation: long-running funding-formula disputes.

pros: Pharma cluster (J&J, Merck, BMS) + financial services NYC-adjacent.
Highest median household income among large states.
Port Newark = busiest East Coast port for container traffic.
cons: Largest per-capita net contribution to federal government — pays $258 B more than gets back (cumulative 2014-23).
Highest property tax in nation; pension underfunding compounds tax pressure.
Atlantic City casino decline + Superstorm Sandy recovery still incomplete.

pros: Boston = biotech + health innovation cluster #1 (Kendall Square, Longwood).
Harvard + MIT anchor research ecosystem.
Highest US bachelor's-degree attainment rate.
cons: Highest cost of living among 50 states; affordability problems mirror Bay Area.
Aging population pressure on long-term care.
Federal contribution skew — receives roughly half what it pays in federal taxes.

pros: TSMC + Intel chip fabs being built — semiconductor renaissance.
Phoenix metro fastest-growing large metro.
Tourism + retirees + cross-border trade with Mexico.
cons: Water (Colorado River compact) — long-term existential constraint.
Power grid summer-load stress + record heat events.
Border immigration enforcement = recurring federal/state friction.

pros: No state income tax. Nashville healthcare + music + tech corridor.
Tier-1 auto manufacturing (Nissan, VW, GM Ultium plant).
Memphis = FedEx hub + Mississippi-river logistics.
cons: Lowest state-spending-per-capita on K-12 in the South.
Rural east + west TN economic divide with Nashville/Chattanooga.
Healthcare-cost inflation despite hospital cluster — coverage gap (no Medicaid expansion).

pros: Manufacturing — steel (Gary), pharma (Lilly), auto + aerospace.
Highest manufacturing share of GDP in the US.
Stable fiscal management; balanced budget reserves consistently.
cons: Wage growth lagged national average for 2 decades.
Educational attainment below regional peers; brain-drain to Chicago.
Manufacturing concentration = high cyclical exposure.

pros: St. Louis = Boeing defense + Anheuser-Busch + AB InBev legacy + agtech.
Kansas City = animal-health corridor + commodity exchange.
Logistics hub — central US distribution.
Workforce relatively low-cost vs east/west coasts.
cons: Public-services investment among lowest in Midwest.
Rural Ozarks economy stagnant; opioid burden.
Recent attacks on Kansas City local control = federal-state friction.

pros: DC adjacency = federal employee concentration + lobbyist + contractor base.
NIH + Johns Hopkins + UMD = research-medical anchor.
Port of Baltimore + Chesapeake Bay seafood economy.
cons: Federal concentration = budget-cycle shock exposure.
Highest income inequality after CT/NY.
Baltimore population loss + civic-government dysfunction long-running.

pros: Dairy + manufacturing + paper. Madison biotech cluster.
Foxconn LCD plant (eventually scaled back) symbolizes manufacturing-incentive scramble.
Lake Michigan freshwater + Great Lakes shipping.
cons: Population aging + outmigration to Twin Cities + Chicago.
Foxconn underperformance damages state credibility on incentive deals.
Gerrymandering litigation = governance friction.

pros: Denver tech + aerospace + Lockheed Martin + Ball Aerospace.
Highest education attainment in interior west.
Cannabis legalization revenue + tourism + ski economy.
cons: Water (Colorado River) + wildfire risk increasing.
Cost of living + housing affordability spiking.
TABOR (Taxpayer Bill of Rights) constrains revenue growth — state-local fiscal squeeze.

pros: Fortune 500 density: UnitedHealth, Target, 3M, Best Buy, Cargill, US Bank.
Highest civic-engagement + voter-turnout in US.
Mayo Clinic + UMN = healthcare research anchor.
cons: Cold climate retains workforce challenges vs sunbelt.
Mining + iron range economic transition incomplete.
Twin Cities / Greater Minnesota political-fiscal divide.

pros: BMW + Volvo + Boeing 787 + Mercedes-Benz Vans = auto manufacturing wave.
Charleston port + tourism + retirees.
Boeing 787 final assembly.
cons: Hurricane exposure (Hugo legacy, Florence recent).
Rural lowcountry economic stagnation outside coastal cities.
School funding equity disputes; lagging educational attainment.

pros: Auto OEMs (Mercedes, Honda, Hyundai, Toyota) made AL Detroit of South.
Huntsville space + defense (NASA Marshall, Redstone Arsenal).
Mobile aerospace (Airbus A320 final-assembly line).
cons: Low educational attainment + persistent poverty in Black Belt counties.
Federal Voting Rights Act litigation = persistent federal-state friction.
Healthcare access — Medicaid non-expansion costs lives.

pros: Petrochemical corridor + LNG export + Gulf oil.
Port of New Orleans + Mississippi River traffic.
Unique civil-law system + Cajun economy + tourism.
cons: Net recipient — receives more federal $ than pays.
Hurricane / coastal-erosion existential exposure (Katrina, Ida).
Education attainment + health outcomes consistently bottom-quintile.

pros: Bourbon + horse industry + Toyota/Ford manufacturing.
Coal-to-EV transition supported by federal IIJA + IRA.
Louisville logistics (UPS Worldport) + Lexington healthcare.
cons: Coal industry decline → eastern Kentucky stagnation.
Health outcomes bottom-quintile (obesity, smoking, opioid).
Pension funding among worst in nation (KRS).

pros: Intel campus + Nike + Columbia Sportswear HQ.
No sales tax (unique in US — relies heavily on income tax).
Portland tech cluster + sustainability industry.
cons: Portland public-safety + homelessness = recurring federal attention.
Forest fire fiscal exposure + watershed risk.
Voter Bill of Rights constraints on tax structure.

pros: Oil + gas + wind = energy diversification.
Tinker AFB + agricultural sector + bioscience.
Tribal sovereignty economy (gaming + service centers).
cons: Energy commodity volatility = revenue swings.
Education attainment lags; teacher walkouts 2018.
McGirt v. Oklahoma = major federal-tribal jurisdiction restructuring.

pros: Insurance HQ (Aetna, Travelers, Hartford). Hedge fund cluster in Greenwich.
Submarine + defense (EB at Groton).
Highest household income median.
cons: Largest per-capita net donor — pays $33 B+ above receipts annually.
Population growth stalled for decade; lost House seat.
Pension funding among worst nationally.

pros: Silicon Slopes — fastest tech growth outside coasts.
Lowest unemployment + youngest median age.
Outdoor recreation + ski + tourism + fintech (eBay, Pluralsight, Domo).
cons: Great Salt Lake drying — ecological + economic risk.
Air quality (Wasatch Front inversion) — public health cost.
Housing affordability + cost-of-living surging.

pros: Corn / soybean / ethanol — agricultural backbone.
Insurance + financial services (Principal, Wells Fargo back office).
Tier-1 ag-tech cluster anchored by ISU.
cons: Climate volatility = farm income volatility.
Population stagnation; rural depopulation.
Caucus relevance declining → less political leverage.

pros: No state income tax. Tesla Gigafactory + lithium mining (Thacker Pass).
Tourism + gaming + entertainment (Vegas, Reno).
Closest non-CA tech alternative for Bay Area firms.
cons: Tourism cyclicality — COVID exposed concentration risk.
Water (Colorado River + Lake Mead) existential.
Education attainment near bottom; per-pupil spending low.

pros: Walmart + Tyson Foods + Dillard's HQs — corporate concentration.
Crystal Bridges + emerging Northwest Arkansas cultural cluster.
Low cost of living + steady migration from neighbors.
cons: Lowest median household income; education attainment lags.
Delta region poverty persistent.
Healthcare outcomes near bottom; Medicaid policy churn.

pros: Aerospace (Wichita = 'Air Capital of the World') + agriculture.
Logistics hub (BNSF + JBHunt + intermodal).
Energy + commodity exchange.
cons: Brownback tax-cut experiment (2012-17) damaged fiscal credibility.
Rural depopulation + farm-bill dependence.
Aerospace concentration risk (Spirit AeroSystems on Boeing).

pros: Federal subsidization keeps fiscal balance functional.
Casino corridor + Gulf Coast tourism + shipbuilding (Pascagoula).
Nissan + Toyota auto plants (Canton, Blue Springs).
cons: Biggest net recipient as % of GDP — ~3:1 ratio of federal transfers to taxes paid.
Lowest median household income + worst health outcomes.
Capital city Jackson water infrastructure failure (2022) symbolic of state-capacity collapse.

pros: Corn + beef + Berkshire Hathaway HQ (Buffett).
Stable conservative fiscal management — no income tax volatility shocks.
Strong agricultural research + Husker workforce.
cons: Population stagnation + youth outmigration.
Climate-change exposure on agriculture (2019 floods).
Healthcare consolidation costs rising.

pros: Fastest-growing state by % population.
Boise tech + Micron semi + Albertsons + Idaho National Lab.
Outmigration destination for Californians + Pacific NW.
cons: Water (Snake River) + wildfire risk increasing.
Housing affordability spiking — 50%+ increase in Boise prices.
Public-services infrastructure not keeping pace with population growth.

pros: Tourism + military bases + unique island agriculture.
Strategic Pacific position — Indo-Pacific Command HQ.
Tightly diversified across hospitality, defense, ag.
cons: Highest cost of living among 50 states.
Climate exposure (sea level + wildfire — Lahaina 2023).
Population growth stagnant; outmigration to mainland.

pros: No state income tax + no sales tax — Live Free Or Die.
Boston commuter belt — high earners, low cost vs MA.
BAE Systems + defense + tech.
cons: Property tax burden among highest in US.
Aging population.
Boston-spillover housing pressure.

pros: Lobster + tourism + Bath Iron Works (DDG-51 destroyers).
Pulp + paper transitioning to bioproducts.
Oldest median age — drives healthcare market.
cons: Aging population = workforce shortage everywhere.
Population concentrated in southern coast; rural north stagnant.
Federal Medicaid + safety-net dependence.

pros: Outdoor recreation + tourism + agriculture.
Bakken shale northern extension.
Rocky Mountain timber + grazing economy.
cons: Wildfire smoke + insurance availability shrinking.
Sparse population = high cost per capita services.
Out-of-state buyers spiking land prices, displacing locals.

pros: Court of Chancery + business-friendly law = most Fortune 500s incorporated here.
DuPont legacy + chemicals + banking + healthcare.
Strategic port + I-95 corridor.
cons: Wilmington banking concentration risk.
Smallest state vulnerable to chain-of-events shocks.
Sea-level rise + coastal exposure.

pros: Brown + RISD + URI = strong R1 universities in compact geography.
Naval Station Newport + defense + biotech.
Tourism + maritime + Newport mansions cultural anchor.
cons: Smallest state in area; population concentrated tightly.
Aging population + outmigration pressures.
Public pension funding stressed.

pros: No state income tax + ultra-favorable trust laws → financial-services magnet.
Mount Rushmore + Black Hills tourism + Sturgis rally.
Agriculture + ethanol + meatpacking.
cons: Population sparse; service delivery costly per capita.
Indigenous reservation poverty among worst in US.
Trust + secrecy laws make state target of FinCEN scrutiny.

pros: Bakken shale = single-state oil bonanza.
Agriculture + bonus to Legacy Fund (oil royalties).
Sparse pop + commodity exports = volatile but high per-capita income.
cons: Oil commodity volatility = revenue swings.
Workforce shortage chronic.
Climate exposure + flood + dust events.

pros: Highest federal-funds per-capita ratio.
Oil royalties → Permanent Fund Dividend ($1,300+/yr to every resident).
Defense + extractive industry + fisheries.
cons: Oil-price-dependent revenue; declining ANS production.
Workforce + cost-of-living among nation's highest.
Climate-change exposure: permafrost + sea-ice loss + erosion.

pros: Ben & Jerry's + tourism + maple syrup + tech (UVM).
Burlington as small-city quality-of-life leader.
Strong public-services investment per capita.
cons: Population aging + outmigration.
Tax base small relative to public-services ambitions.
Climate flooding (2023 vermont floods).

pros: Coal + oil + uranium + bentonite — extractive-industry concentrated.
Lowest population state — congressional leverage per capita strong.
Tourism (Yellowstone, Grand Teton).
cons: Coal industry collapse → structural revenue + employment loss.
Workforce shortage extreme.
Sparse public services.

pros: Federal pipeline + infrastructure spending direct via Manchin/Capito.
Outdoor recreation pivot post-coal (New River Gorge NP).
Aluminum + chemicals + emerging tech (Marshall U + WVU).
cons: Biggest population decline 2010-23 of any state.
Coal industry collapse → opioid + economic depression.
Health outcomes consistently bottom-3.

pros: Permian Basin oil → suddenly major state revenue source.
Federal labs (Los Alamos, Sandia) + Holloman AFB.
Indigenous + Hispanic cultural economy + tourism.
cons: Highest federal-dependence ratio.
Education + health outcomes bottom-5.
Water (Rio Grande + Colorado River).
